# exasol Provider

Manage Exasol database resources: users, roles, schemas, connections, and privileges.

## Example Usage

```terraform
provider "exasol" {
  host     = "exasol.example.com"
  port     = 8563
  user     = "sys"
  password = "exasol"

  # Set to false for self-signed certificates (e.g. Docker containers)
  validate_server_certificate = true
}

# PAT token authentication (detected automatically by exa_pat_ prefix)
provider "exasol" {
  alias    = "pat"
  host     = "exasol.example.com"
  user     = "my_user"
  password = "exa_pat_abc123..."
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host` (String) Exasol host (DNS or IP).
- `password` (String, Sensitive) Exasol password or Personal Access Token (PAT). Tokens starting with exa_pat_ are detected automatically.
- `user` (String) Exasol username.

### Optional

- `port` (Number) Exasol port. Default 8563.
- `validate_server_certificate` (Boolean) Validate server TLS certificate. Default true.
